name: turbomem
description: Give Claude a private, local long-term memory that remembers things about you across conversations.
long_description:
turbomem adds a persistent memory to Claude Desktop. Claude can remember durable facts about you — your preferences, projects, and goals — and recall them in future chats. Everything is stored locally on your computer in a database on disk; nothing is uploaded anywhere except the AI provider you choose to extract and embed your notes.

After installing, just pick a provider (OpenAI, Google Gemini, or Anthropic) and paste one API key. Then talk to Claude normally — it will save and recall memories on its own.

tools.name: remember
tools.description: Save durable information about the user to long-term memory.

tools.name: recall
tools.description: Search long-term memory for information relevant to a query.

tools.name: forget
tools.description: Delete a single memory by id.

tools.name: forget_everything
tools.description: Permanently delete all stored memories (requires confirmation).

provider.title: AI provider
provider.description: Which AI provider to use for reading and organizing your memories. Type one of: openai, google, anthropic.

api_key.title: API key
api_key.description: Paste the API key for the provider you chose above (an OpenAI, Google Gemini, or Anthropic key).

user_id.title: Memory profile name
user_id.description: A label for this memory profile. Leave as 'me' unless you keep separate memory sets.

data_dir.title: Storage folder
data_dir.description: Where your memories are stored on disk. The default is shared with the turbomem CLI.
